decisions.reason: The Figshare record is a single supplementary file for a paper that explicitly self-describes as replicating the effect of emotion on automatic intergroup attitudes. It is not a reusable corpus/database source-family row.

route_payload.replication_relation_evidence: The source title says 'Replicating the effect of emotion on automatic intergroup attitudes'.

decisions.notes: Figshare API exposed one DOCX file, 40359_2020_414_MOESM1_ESM.docx.

decisions.reason: This is a supplementary appendix for a statistical-interpretation methods paper. It may be general methodological context, but it does not expose original/replication pair rows or a replication source family.
decisions.notes: Figshare API exposed one PDF appendix file, 12874_2020_1105_MOESM1_ESM.pdf.

decisions.reason: This is a search-strategy supplement for the REPRISE protocol. It is protocol/context material, not a result-bearing source-family artifact or pair table.
decisions.notes: Figshare API exposed one PDF search-strategy file, 13643_2021_1670_MOESM1_ESM.pdf.

decisions.reason: The Figshare record is a qualitative supplement containing key illustrative quotes for a Nepal mental-wellbeing care-pathways article. It has no Figure 1 replication-pair evidence.
decisions.notes: Figshare API exposed one DOCX file, 13033_2020_433_MOESM1_ESM.docx.

decisions.reason: The Figshare record is a policy/framework supplement for the same Nepal mental-wellbeing care-pathways article. It is not a replication or corpus/database artifact.
decisions.notes: Figshare API exposed one DOCX file, 13033_2020_433_MOESM2_ESM.docx.

decisions.reason: The Zenodo record is a code snapshot for the Fischer et al. (2003) multi-lab replication main task. It is an individual RRR-style replication artifact, not a new corpus/database root.

route_payload.replication_relation_evidence: The record title says 'Fischer et al (2003) multi-lab replication main task'.

decisions.notes: Zenodo exposes one zip file, ljcolling/FischerRRR-eyetracking-v1.0.zip.

decisions.reason: This Zenodo record is for a software-engineering technical paper dataset about LLM summarization for legacy program documentation. The word replication appears to refer to a package/dataset route, not an independent replication of scientific findings.
decisions.notes: Zenodo API showed no files on the record during this check.

decisions.reason: The Dataverse title is a generic 'Replication Package' for an economics VAT pass-through article. The description is an original empirical paper reproduction package, not a replication/follow-up source-family object.
decisions.notes: Applied ordinary replication-package demotion gate.

decisions.reason: ReScience C is a journal/source-family contents page for computational replication articles, with many article records and code/review metadata. It is a reusable source-family object rather than a single paper.
decisions.notes: Promote as a source-family candidate; table-level integration still requires extracting original-paper links and comparable metrics from individual records.

decisions.reason: The Nature Ecology and Evolution article is a large in-silico replicability analysis rather than a direct independent original-versus-replication corpus. It may be useful as cross-field context or a manual claim-replication target, but should not advance as a Figure 1 pair source without a stricter mapping review.
decisions.notes: Crossref links VOR PDF/HTML, but the semantics are in-silico/meta-analytic rather than direct replication-pair rows.

decisions.reason: The Nature Computational Science article title identifies a large-scale replication of scenario-based experiments. It is plausibly a multi-study source-family candidate, but source inventory must confirm whether article data or supplements expose original/replication mapping and usable result fields.
decisions.notes: Keep as an existing candidate, not plot-ready evidence.

decisions.reason: The stale OSF 3hjpf lead is the Olson and Fazio registered replication source family; the canonical public project is OSF hs32y and the article DOI is 10.1177/0956797620968526. It should be kept but deduplicated to that canonical route.
decisions.notes: OSF API returned 404 for 3hjpf and public metadata for hs32y.

decisions.reason: This Registered Replication Report is a multi-lab source-family candidate: the abstract reports 23 laboratories and N=2141 for a standardized ego-depletion replication protocol. It should stay for source inventory and table extraction review.
decisions.notes: Crossref exposes Sage PDF and full XML links.

decisions.reason: This is a critique/reanalysis of Many Labs 2 culture-moderation methods. Many Labs 2 itself is already the source-family candidate; this preprint is contextual and should not create a separate Figure 1 source-family row.
decisions.notes: Crossref abstract explicitly frames it as a reanalysis/critique of ML2.

decisions.reason: This is a scoping review and live-table resource about reproducibility metrics and large-scale projects. It is useful for context or seed discovery, but it is not itself an original/replication pair corpus.
decisions.notes: Crossref exposes Royal Society PDF and XML links.

decisions.reason: The abstract explicitly says the study performed a large-scale replication of Kosfeld et al. (2005) and pooled it with another replication. This is a single replication paper/workflow, not a broad source-family database.

route_payload.replication_relation_evidence: The abstract says 'We performed a large-scale replication (n = 211) of Kosfeld et al. (2005)' and describes pooling with Declerck et al. (2020).

decisions.notes: Crossref lists Elsevier XML/text API routes.

decisions.reason: The article title explicitly self-describes as a multi-site replication. No evidence in the current metadata shows a reusable multi-row corpus/database table, so it should be handled as an individual replication paper.

route_payload.replication_relation_evidence: The title says 'Administering a victim impact curriculum to inmates: a multi-site replication'.

decisions.notes: Crossref did not expose an abstract, only bibliographic metadata and a publisher PDF link.

decisions.reason: This article discusses replication and open science in cognitive science of religion and mentions the Cross-Cultural Religious Replication Project, but it is not itself a result-bearing source family or pair table.
decisions.notes: Crossref exposes a VOR PDF link and abstract-style context.

decisions.reason: This eLife article defines the Reproducibility Project: Cancer Biology source family, identifying 50 high-impact cancer biology articles and planned replication studies. It should be retained but deduplicated with the later RPCB result-data source-family records.
decisions.notes: Crossref exposes eLife PDF/XML links; current cluster review already has RPCB inventory routes.

decisions.reason: This is a single genetics association/meta-analysis paper using the language of variant replication across cohorts. It does not provide a Figure 1 source-family object linking published original findings to independent replication/follow-up studies under the current gate.
decisions.notes: This applies the genetics validation strict-gate caution; it is not a judgment that the article lacks statistical results.
